  "findings": [
    "Receptacles are wired using push-in backstab connections with 14 AWG solid copper on a 15 A circuit."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Advise the homeowner that the installation is code-compliant and passes inspection, but explain the reliability benefits of side-terminal screw or back-wire clamp connections."
  ],
  "rationale": "The installation passes inspection because NEC 110.14(B) and UL 498 permit push-in (backstab) terminations for 14 AWG solid copper wire on 15 A branch circuits when the device is listed for it. However, experienced electricians typically prefer side-screw terminals or screw-actuated back-wire clamps because backstab spring-tension connections have a higher rate of failure over time due to thermal cycling, reduced surface contact area, and mechanical fatigue.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14(B)",
    "UL 498",
    "NEC 110.3(B)"
  ]
}
